session.name: MPI-64 RunPod Remote Engine — Phase 1 design, Phase 2 wrapper+live Pod proven, Parallel Batch #1 (backend client+secret store) done

goal.original: Implement MPI-64 RunPod Remote Engine: run ComfyUI generation on a user's RunPod Secure Cloud GPU from the Cubric Vision desktop app, via a Cubric-owned hidden Pod template + token-auth HTTP wrapper, network-volume persistence, app-managed lifecycle, GPU/PyTorch compatibility gates.
goal.summary: Phase 1 design (7 research docs) complete and decisions resolved with the user. Phase 2 wrapper built (full contract, 24/24 local verify) and PROVEN END-TO-END on a real RunPod A4000 Pod through the proxy (image pulled, ComfyUI loaded on GPU, token auth + volume upload worked). Parallel Batch #1 (backend RunPod client + safeStorage secret store) implemented inline and verified (mocked + live GraphQL). Next: smoke-test the app with npm start, then Batch #2 (Comfy boundary adapter — the risky core change).

completed: Phase 1 — all 6 design decisions: transport-topology, wrapper-api-contract, template-distribution, volume-manifest-schema, pod-lifecycle-policy, secret-storage (+ bonus gpu-availability-and-datacenter.md)
Phase 2 step 1 — wrapper prototype then FULL contract, 24/24 verify PASS local
Phase 2 step 2 — image on GHCR + RunPod template created + live Pod reached ready

Parallel Batch #1 — backend RunPod client + secret store (NOT yet a checkbox in plan.md; tracked in events.jsonl)
pending: Run npm start to smoke-test secrets:* IPC + fork bridge in the real Electron app (USER is doing this now)
Parallel Batch #2 — Comfy boundary adapter: route comfyController.js/commandExecutor.js to the wrapper proxy in remote mode, local mode BYTE-IDENTICAL (the risky core change)
Parallel Batch #3 — Settings RunPod section + master toggle + advisory pop-up + API-key password field + GPU/DC availability picker + remote-mode boot gate (shell.js)
Phase 3 — model download onto volume (start with ONE SDXL checkpoint, NOT WAN), write volume manifest, bundle versioning
Phase 4 — end-to-end remote generation (SDXL image first)
Phase 5 — hardening, secret-hygiene audit (add global logger redaction hook), docs

All design decisions captured in research/*.md (7 docs) and reflected in plan

Push the UPDATED full-contract image to GHCR — blocked by transient GHCR 502 this session; retry: docker push ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.1.0 (original live-tested image is already public on GHCR)
Consider archiving the test Pod or keeping for next test (user chose KEEP both Pod+volume)

next_action.description: User is running npm start to smoke-test the app UI + the new secrets:* IPC and fork bridge (Batch #1). After confirming the app boots and IPC works, proceed to Parallel Batch #2 (Comfy boundary adapter). BRIEF BEFORE CODE per CLAUDE.md: read .claude/rules/comfy_injection.md + events.md + the transport-topology.md and wrapper-api-contract.md research docs, then brief the user and wait for 'go'. Batch #2 must keep LOCAL mode byte-identical — remote selected through a narrow adapter only.

key_decisions: Architecture: Secure-Cloud-only RunPod Pods from a Cubric-owned PUBLIC template (option a — verified RunPod public templates deploy cross-account with any user key). Image public on GHCR. No raw ComfyUI exposed; no arbitrary remote URLs; no Community Cloud.

Wrapper runtime = Python/FastAPI (OneTrainer precedent). Permanent home = private MadPonyInteractive/mpi-ci (cloned to c:/AI/Mpi/mpi-ci). Scratch dev copy at c:/AI/Mpi/cubric-remote-wrapper (has venv + verify.py + fake_comfy.py + .secrets/runpod.env).

Transport = BACKEND PROXY: Express holds the wrapper token server-side, forwards to the RunPod proxy; the ONE exception is renderer WSS-direct for binary preview frames. Token never in renderer storage.

Secret storage = Option B: Electron safeStorage when available, AES-256-GCM derived-key file fallback + weakEncryption warning at save time otherwise. NEVER lock Linux users out (user decision). API key field = MpiInput password mode.

Lifecycle: stop-on-quit (not delete); network-volume delete is a first-class user action (needed to switch DC/GPU family, since a volume locks to one DC). Idle watchdog 15 min inference (12 min noted for future LoRA training).

Connection model: NO Connect button. Settings RunPod master toggle (off default) -> advisory pop-up on app open ('RunPod active, ComfyUI start uses your credits') -> Pod starts at the EXISTING comfy:ready boot trigger, routed to remote.

GPU availability IS fetchable via GraphQL dataCenters{gpuAvailability{available gpuTypeId stockStatus}} — beats OneTrainer's link-only. available=deploy gate, stockStatus(High/Medium/Low/null)=likelihood hint, null != unavailable.

SECURITY FIX this session: GraphQL key was in the URL query -> would leak via err.message -> app.log -> bug report. Moved to Authorization header + added redactSecret() (api_key=/rpa_/Bearer). Confirmed live.
constraints: NEVER commit to git unless user explicitly asks (CLAUDE.md). Work is uncommitted on branch RunPod.
Branch RunPod is behind master by 3 docs-only commits (README/cleanup). Rebase onto master before the final PR; near-zero conflict risk.
Sub-agents start COLD — brief them with Critical Rules Snapshot + relevant rule briefings (use mpi-brief-rule). User chose INLINE-SEQUENTIAL execution for app code, not sub-agents.

Windows curl flakes on POST-multipart through the RunPod Cloudflare proxy (http=000) — use Python+browser-UA for remote tests. RunPod proxy + API are behind Cloudflare: default UA gets 403 error 1010, MUST send a browser User-Agent.
Docker data root moved to D: (C drive was ~8GB free). Docker engine v29.5.3. Docker bin: '/c/Program Files/Docker/Docker/resources/bin' (add to PATH).

RunPod secrets live in c:/AI/Mpi/cubric-remote-wrapper/.secrets/runpod.env (GITIGNORED, NOT a git repo): RUNPOD_API_KEY (existing Key1, R&W), RUNPOD_VOLUME_ID=cfwqthmdrg, RUNPOD_DATACENTER=EUR-IS-1, RUNPOD_TEMPLATE_ID=ejrwtsej15, RUNPOD_POD_ID=k8pr35yto3xy7n. NEVER paste the key in chat or logs.

Standing RunPod resources (user chose KEEP): Pod k8pr35yto3xy7n (STOPPED, A4000, EUR-IS-1) — restart for more tests; 150GB volume cubric-vision-vol cfwqthmdrg (EUR-IS-1, bills ~$10.50/mo storage); template ejrwtsej15; image ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.1.0 (public).
EUR-IS-1 is the chosen DC: has RTX 5090 (real use) + A4000 + L4 (cheap test) + storage. Volume is locked to EUR-IS-1.
Batch #1 secrets:* IPC + fork bridge verified by LOGIC test only, NOT yet by a real npm start run — that is the immediate next validation (user doing it).
DOCS WEBSITE PUSH BLOCK still applies (unrelated to MPI-64). Never push c:/AI/Mpi/Cubric Studio (Docs).
